bullets.text: Russian strikes killed 11 people and set a historic cathedral ablaze in Kyiv, following a Ukrainian drone attack in the Russian city of Tula that killed three.
Attacks on education globally have surged by 40%, with a study reporting cases in 83 countries and at least 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted, or arrested.

An investigation found that global brands like Amazon and Sony may be using coltan, a mineral found in phones, from supply chains controlled by M23 rebels accused of atrocities in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
A marine biologist has stated that culling sharks would not reduce the risk of attacks due to their migratory patterns, as debate continues on how to improve safety at Sydney beaches.

The clearing of koala habitat at a Brisbane school has sparked outrage among advocates and raised questions about the effectiveness of the federal government's recent changes to national environment laws.
An official from a One Nation branch has been found to have made racist posts online, defending Hitler Youth and referring to Aboriginal people as 'stone age'.

Global stocks surged and average US petrol prices fell below $4, following an interim US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, easing inflation concerns and positively impacting investor sentiment.

Fox announced its acquisition of streaming platform Roku for $22 billion, a move that will make the combined entity the third-largest in US television by viewing share and reflects significant shifts in the media landscape.
Nvidia Corp. sold $25 billion of high-grade bonds, joining other tech giants in a wave of jumbo debt offerings as investors show strong interest in the artificial intelligence sector.
